test_cases.description: Verifies that on first startup, when config.toml does not exist, the CLI creates $HOME/.af/ with 0700 and $HOME/.af/config.toml with 0600 containing a comment header and hub_url = "".
preconditions: $HOME is set to a writable temp directory controlled by the test
test_cases.input: CLI startup routine is invoked (e.g. ensureConfigFile() or equivalent initialization function)
test_cases.expected: $HOME/.af/ directory exists with permissions 0700; $HOME/.af/config.toml exists with permissions 0600; file content includes a comment header and the line `hub_url = ""`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
call ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
assert dirExists(homeDir + "/.af/")
assert dirPermissions(homeDir + "/.af/") == 0700
assert fileExists(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
assert filePermissions(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == 0600
content = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
assert content contains "hub_url = \"\""
assert content contains "# Client configuration for afc"

test_cases.description: Verifies that when $HOME/.af/config.toml already exists, the CLI startup routine makes no changes to the file or directory.
test_cases.expected: $HOME/.af/config.toml content is unchanged after startup; no new files are created; directory permissions are not altered
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
existingContent = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
existingModTime = modTime(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
call ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
assert readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == existingContent
assert modTime(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == existingModTime

test_cases.description: Verifies that permissions on pre-existing $HOME/.af/ and config.toml are not modified by the CLI startup routine.
test_cases.expected: $HOME/.af/ retains permissions 0755 and $HOME/.af/config.toml retains permissions 0644 — neither is changed to 0700/0600
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
call ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
assert dirPermissions(homeDir + "/.af/") == 0755
assert filePermissions(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == 0644

test_cases.description: Verifies that the config file is parsed into a struct with hub_url (string) and api_key (string) top-level fields using the BurntSushi TOML decoder.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists and contains valid TOML with hub_url and api_key set
test_cases.expected: Decoded struct has HubURL == "https://hub.example.com" and APIKey == "my-project"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.HubURL == "https://hub.example.com"
assert cfg.APIKey == "my-project"

test_cases.description: Verifies that [keys.<workspace_slug>] TOML sections are parsed into key entries with key_id, token, and optional label fields.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists with a [keys.my-project] section containing key_id, token, and label
test_cases.input:
config.toml content includes:
```toml
[keys.my-project]
key_id = "a1b2c3"
token = "af_a1b2c3_secret"
label = "dev laptop"
```
test_cases.expected: cfg.Keys["my-project"].KeyID == "a1b2c3", cfg.Keys["my-project"].Token == "af_a1b2c3_secret", cfg.Keys["my-project"].Label == "dev laptop"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].KeyID == "a1b2c3"
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].Token == "af_a1b2c3_secret"
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].Label == "dev laptop"

test_cases.description: Verifies that an empty string for hub_url or api_key in the config file is treated as unset and resolution falls through to the next precedence level.
test_cases.input: No --hub-url flag, no AF_HUB_URL env var; config file has hub_url = ""
test_cases.expected: resolveHubURL returns an error (falls through to error level, not an empty string result)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
// hub_url is empty string
result, err = resolveHubURL(flag="", env="", cfg=cfg)
assert err != nil
assert result == ""
// api_key is empty string
result, err = resolveAPIKey(flag="", env="", cfg=cfg)
assert err != nil

test_cases.description: Verifies the full hub URL resolution precedence: flag > AF_HUB_URL env var > config file hub_url > error.
test_cases.input: Test four scenarios in order: (1) flag="https://flag.example.com", env="https://env.example.com"; (2) flag="", env="https://env.example.com"; (3) flag="", env=""; (4) flag="", env="", config hub_url = ""
test_cases.expected: (1) returns "https://flag.example.com"; (2) returns "https://env.example.com"; (3) returns "https://config.example.com"; (4) returns error

test_cases.description: Verifies the full API key token resolution precedence: --api-key flag > AF_HUB_API_KEY env var > config file keys lookup > error.
test_cases.input: Four scenarios: (1) flag token provided; (2) env var token provided; (3) neither flag nor env, config has valid key; (4) no source has a valid key
test_cases.expected: (1) returns flag value as token; (2) returns env var as token; (3) returns "af_abc_secret" from config; (4) returns error

test_cases.description: Verifies that the error message when hub URL or API key cannot be resolved mentions $HOME/.af/config.toml, the relevant flag, and the relevant env var.
test_cases.expected: Error message string contains references to "$HOME/.af/config.toml" (or expanded path), "--hub-url", and "AF_HUB_URL"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
_, err = resolveHubURL(flag="", env="", cfg.HubURL="")
assert err != nil
assert errorMessage(err) contains "config.toml"
assert errorMessage(err) contains "--hub-url"
assert errorMessage(err) contains "AF_HUB_URL"

test_cases.description: Verifies that config file mutations write to a temp file in $HOME/.af/ via os.CreateTemp then rename atomically via os.Rename.
test_cases.input: Trigger any config mutation (e.g. setDefaultKey("my-project")) while observing file system calls
test_cases.expected: A temp file is created in $HOME/.af/ before config.toml is updated; config.toml is atomically replaced via rename; no temp file remains after the operation
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// instrument os.CreateTemp and os.Rename with test doubles
spy = newFileOpSpy()
call setDefaultKey(homeDir, "my-project", spy)
assert spy.createTempCalled == true
assert spy.createTempDir == homeDir + "/.af/"
assert spy.renameCalled == true
assert spy.renameDestination == homeDir + "/.af/config.toml"
// verify temp file is gone after mutation
assert NOT fileExists(spy.tempFilePath)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the temp file used for atomic config writes is created with permissions 0600 before config content is written to it.
test_cases.input: Trigger a config mutation and capture the temp file's permissions before rename
test_cases.expected: Temp file has permissions 0600 at the moment it is written
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
call mutateConfig(homeDir, newConfig, capturePermsBefore=true)
assert capturedTempFilePermissions == 0600

test_cases.description: Verifies that after a successful afc login, a [keys._login] section with key_id, token, and label="login" is written to config.toml and api_key is set to _login.
test_cases.input: `afc login --hub-url https://hub.example.com` with OAuth callback returning api_key
test_cases.expected: config.toml contains [keys._login] with key_id="0011aabb", token="af_0011aabb_secret", label="login"; top-level api_key="_login"; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
runCLI(["login", "--hub-url", "https://hub.example.com"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.APIKey == "_login"
assert cfg.Keys["_login"].KeyID == "0011aabb"
assert cfg.Keys["_login"].Token == "af_0011aabb_secret"
assert cfg.Keys["_login"].Label == "login"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc login writes hub_url to the config file when hub_url is currently empty.
preconditions: OAuth callback mock returns valid api_key
test_cases.expected: After login, config.toml has hub_url = "https://hub.example.com"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// pre-condition: cfg.HubURL == ""
runCLI(["login", "--hub-url", "https://hub.example.com"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.HubURL == "https://hub.example.com"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc login does not overwrite an already-set hub_url in the config file.
preconditions: OAuth callback mock returns valid api_key
test_cases.expected: After login, config.toml still has hub_url = "https://existing.example.com" (not overwritten)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// pre-condition: cfg.HubURL == "https://existing.example.com"
runCLI(["login", "--hub-url", "https://new.example.com"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.HubURL == "https://existing.example.com"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys create adds a [keys.<workspace_slug>] section to config.toml with key_id, token, and label from the server response.
preconditions: Mock API returns a new key with key_id, token, label for workspace "my-project"
test_cases.expected: config.toml gains a [keys.my-project] section with correct key_id, token, and label; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
runCLI(["keys", "create", "--workspace", "my-project"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert "my-project" in cfg.Keys
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].KeyID != ""
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].Token != ""

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys refresh updates the token of the matching [keys.*] section in config.toml identified by key_id.
preconditions: Mock API returns new token "af_a1b2c3_new" for key_id="a1b2c3"
test_cases.expected: config.toml [keys.my-project].token is updated to "af_a1b2c3_new"; key_id and label are unchanged; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
runCLI(["keys", "refresh", "a1b2c3"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].Token == "af_a1b2c3_new"
assert cfg.Keys["my-project"].KeyID == "a1b2c3"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys revoke removes the [keys.*] section matching the given key_id from config.toml.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml has [keys.staging] with key_id="f7e8d9" and api_key set to a different slug
test_cases.expected: [keys.staging] section is removed from config.toml; api_key is not changed (was not the default); exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
runCLI(["keys", "revoke", "f7e8d9"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert "staging" NOT in cfg.Keys

test_cases.description: Verifies that when the revoked key's workspace slug matches the current api_key default, api_key is cleared and a warning is printed to stderr.
test_cases.input: `afc keys revoke a1b2c3` where the revoked key's slug is the current default
test_cases.expected: config.toml api_key is set to empty string; warning printed to stderr mentioning `afc keys default`; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
stderr, _ = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "revoke", "a1b2c3"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.APIKey == ""
assert "my-project" NOT in cfg.Keys
assert stderr contains "afc keys default"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys default sets api_key to the specified workspace_slug when a matching [keys.*] section exists.
test_cases.expected: config.toml api_key is updated to "my-project"; confirmation printed to stdout; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
stdout, _ = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "default", "my-project"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.APIKey == "my-project"
assert stdout contains "my-project"

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys default _login sets api_key to _login when a [keys._login] section exists.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml has [keys._login] with valid key_id and token; api_key = "my-project"
test_cases.expected: config.toml api_key is updated to "_login"; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
runCLI(["keys", "default", "_login"])
assert exitCode == 0
cfg = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert cfg.APIKey == "_login"

test_cases.description: Verifies that the server's POST /api/v1/auth/callback response includes both a user object and an api_key object with key and key_id fields.
preconditions: afc server is running with OAuth configured
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 response body is valid JSON containing {"user": {...}, "api_key": {"key": "af_<key_id>_<secret>", "key_id": "<key_id>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = httpPost("/api/v1/auth/callback", validOAuthPayload)
assert response.statusCode == 200
body = parseJSON(response.body)
assert body.user != null
assert body.api_key != null
assert body.api_key.key matches regex "^af_[a-f0-9]+_"
assert body.api_key.key_id matches regex "^[a-f0-9]+$"

test_cases.description: Verifies that --hub-url, --api-key, AF_HUB_URL, and AF_HUB_API_KEY are still accepted and function with identical semantics as before config file support.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists with hub_url = "https://config.example.com" and a valid key
test_cases.expected: Resolved hub URL is "https://flag.example.com" and resolved token is "af_flag_secret"; config file values are not used
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = resolveCredentials(flag_hub_url="https://flag.example.com", flag_api_key="af_flag_secret", cfg=cfg)
assert result.hubURL == "https://flag.example.com"
assert result.token == "af_flag_secret"

test_cases.description: Verifies that when --hub-url flag or AF_HUB_URL env var is present, hub_url from config file is not used.
test_cases.expected: Returns "https://flag.example.com" without reading config file value
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = resolveHubURL(flag="https://flag.example.com", env="", cfg.HubURL="https://config.example.com")
assert err == nil
assert result == "https://flag.example.com"
// Also test env var override
result, err = resolveHubURL(flag="", env="https://env.example.com", cfg.HubURL="https://config.example.com")
assert err == nil
assert result == "https://env.example.com"

test_cases.description: Verifies that when --api-key flag or AF_HUB_API_KEY env var is present, the config file key lookup is bypassed.
test_cases.input: resolveAPIKey called with flag="af_flag_token" and env="af_env_token"
test_cases.expected: Flag value is returned without reading config; env var value is returned when flag is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = resolveAPIKey(flag="af_flag_token", env="af_env_token", cfg=cfg)
assert err == nil
assert result == "af_flag_token"
// env var path
result, err = resolveAPIKey(flag="", env="af_env_token", cfg=cfg)
assert err == nil
assert result == "af_env_token"

property_tests.description: For any invocation where --hub-url or AF_HUB_URL is set to a non-empty value, the resolved hub URL must always equal the flag or env var value, never the config file value.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random non-empty strings for flag_hub_url or env_hub_url; generate random strings (possibly equal, possibly different) for cfg.HubURL; also generate cases where config has a non-empty hub_url

property_tests.description: For any newly created $HOME/.af/ directory and $HOME/.af/config.toml file, permissions must immediately be 0700 and 0600 respectively.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any writable temp directory used as homeDir (generate multiple distinct temp dirs)
property_tests.invariant_check:
for any homeDir where $HOME/.af/ does not yet exist:
  call ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
  assert dirPermissions(homeDir + "/.af/") == 0700
  assert filePermissions(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == 0600

property_tests.description: For any config mutation operation, at no point does config.toml contain partially written content; the file is always either the complete old or complete new content.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any valid config state and any mutation (setDefaultKey, updateToken, removeKey, writeLoginKey) with random concurrent read of the config file during write

property_tests.description: For any config file where hub_url or api_key is set to an empty string, resolution never short-circuits with an empty string result; it always falls through to the next level.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate config files with hub_url = "" and/or api_key = ""; generate all combinations of flag (empty/non-empty) and env var (empty/non-empty)

property_tests.description: For any afc command that is not login, keys create, keys refresh, keys revoke, or keys default, config.toml must not be modified.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any non-mutating command (e.g. workspace list, keys list, workspace create without key storage) and any initial config state
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each non-mutating command C:
  originalContent = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
  originalModTime = modTime(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
  runCLI(C)
  assert readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == originalContent
  assert modTime(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == originalModTime

property_tests.description: For any workspace_slug argument to afc keys default that has no matching [keys.*] section, api_key in config.toml must not be modified and exit code must be non-zero.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random workspace slug strings that are guaranteed not to appear in the config file's Keys map (e.g. random UUID-like strings); test with any initial api_key value
property_tests.invariant_check:
for any slug NOT in cfg.Keys:
  originalAPIKey = cfg.APIKey
  runCLI(["keys", "default", slug])
  assert exitCode != 0
  assert loadConfig(homeDir).APIKey == originalAPIKey

property_tests.description: Resolution of hub URL and API key terminates in a bounded number of steps (at most 3 checks: flag, env, config) and never loops or retries indefinitely.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any combination of flag (empty/non-empty), env var (empty/non-empty), and config state (valid, empty, missing key), invoke resolve functions and count internal state transitions
property_tests.invariant_check:
for any (flag, env, cfg) input:
  stepsCounter = 0
  result, err = resolveHubURLInstrumented(flag, env, cfg, counter=&stepsCounter)
  assert stepsCounter <= 3  // flag check, env check, config check
  // Same for API key
  stepsCounter2 = 0
  result2, err2 = resolveAPIKeyInstrumented(flag, env, cfg, counter=&stepsCounter2)
  assert stepsCounter2 <= 4  // flag check, env check, api_key slug lookup, keys section lookup

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when $HOME/.af/ cannot be created due to a permission error, the CLI exits with a non-zero status and prints a descriptive error to stderr.
preconditions: Parent directory of $HOME is not writable (or $HOME itself is read-only) so mkdir for $HOME/.af/ will fail
Test uses a temp home dir where .af cannot be mkdir'd (e.g. permissions set to 0500 on homeDir)
edge_case_tests.input: CLI startup routine invoked with homeDir set to a directory where .af/ cannot be created
edge_case_tests.expected: ensureConfigExists returns a non-nil error; error message contains the path and describes the permission failure
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
os.Chmod(homeDir, 0500) // make homeDir read-only
err = ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
assert err != nil
assert errorMessage(err) contains homeDir + "/.af"
os.Chmod(homeDir, 0700) // restore for cleanup

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when config.toml cannot be created (directory not writable), the CLI exits with an error and descriptive stderr message.
edge_case_tests.input: CLI startup routine invoked with $HOME/.af/ not writable
edge_case_tests.expected: ensureConfigExists returns a non-nil error; error message references $HOME/.af/config.toml
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
os.MkdirAll(homeDir + "/.af/", 0500)
err = ensureConfigExists(homeDir)
assert err != nil
assert errorMessage(err) contains "config.toml"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that if config.toml creation fails partway through (simulated write error), any partial file is cleaned up before returning the error.
preconditions: Write to config.toml is interrupted after partial content is written (simulated via a write-limited fake file)
edge_case_tests.input: File write operation fails mid-write during auto-creation
edge_case_tests.expected: Partial $HOME/.af/config.toml is removed; ensureConfigExists returns non-nil error; no partial file remains
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Inject a fake writer that fails after N bytes
err = ensureConfigExistsWithWriter(homeDir, failingWriter)
assert err != nil
assert NOT fileExists(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when config.toml contains invalid TOML, the CLI exits immediately with a non-zero status and a parse error message identifying the file and the issue.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists with invalid TOML content (e.g. `hub_url = [not valid`)
edge_case_tests.input: loadConfig invoked on a malformed config file
edge_case_tests.expected: loadConfig returns a non-nil error; error message contains the file path and a description of the parse failure; program does not silently fall through
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
writeFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml", "hub_url = [not valid")
_, err = loadConfig(homeDir)
assert err != nil
assert errorMessage(err) contains homeDir + "/.af/config.toml"
assert errorMessage(err) contains "parse" OR "decode" OR "TOML"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when api_key references a workspace_slug with no matching [keys.*] section, the value is treated as unset and resolution falls through.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml has api_key = "nonexistent-slug" with no matching [keys.nonexistent-slug] section
edge_case_tests.input: resolveAPIKey called with flag="", env="", cfg.APIKey="nonexistent-slug", cfg.Keys has no "nonexistent-slug" entry
edge_case_tests.expected: resolveAPIKey does not return "nonexistent-slug" as a token; falls through to return an error (since no other source is available)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg.APIKey = "nonexistent-slug"
cfg.Keys = {} // empty
result, err = resolveAPIKey(flag="", env="", cfg=cfg)
assert err != nil
assert result == ""

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when os.Rename fails during an atomic config write, the temp file is removed via deferred cleanup and the original config.toml is left unchanged.
preconditions: os.Rename is stubbed/mocked to return an error
edge_case_tests.input: Atomic write mutation triggered with a mocked rename failure
edge_case_tests.expected: Original config.toml content is unchanged; temp file no longer exists; function returns non-nil error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
originalContent = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
// inject rename failure
err = atomicWriteConfig(homeDir, newConfig, renameFunc=alwaysFailRename)
assert err != nil
assert readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == originalContent
// temp file should be removed
assert count(glob(homeDir + "/.af/tmp*")) == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when os.CreateTemp fails during a config mutation, no changes are made to the original config.toml.
preconditions: os.CreateTemp is stubbed to return an error
edge_case_tests.input: Config mutation triggered with mocked CreateTemp failure
edge_case_tests.expected: Original config.toml content is unchanged; function returns non-nil error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
originalContent = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
err = atomicWriteConfig(homeDir, newConfig, createTempFunc=alwaysFailCreateTemp)
assert err != nil
assert readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == originalContent

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the login callback response is missing the api_key field, the CLI exits with an error and does not modify the config file.
preconditions: Mock server at POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns only {"user": {...}} with no api_key field
edge_case_tests.input: `afc login --hub-url https://hub.example.com` with OAuth callback missing api_key
edge_case_tests.expected: CLI exits with non-zero status; error message on stderr mentions missing credential data; config.toml is unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
originalContent = readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml")
_, stderr = runCLIWithOutput(["login", "--hub-url", "https://hub.example.com"])
assert exitCode != 0
assert stderr contains "api_key" OR stderr contains "credential"
assert readFile(homeDir + "/.af/config.toml") == originalContent

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when afc keys refresh is called for a key_id not present in config.toml, a warning is logged to stderr but the CLI does not exit with an error.
preconditions: Mock API successfully refreshes the remote key
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 0; warning on stderr mentions the key was not found locally in config; config.toml is not modified
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
_, stderr = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "refresh", "deadbeef"])
assert exitCode == 0
assert stderr contains "deadbeef" OR stderr contains "not found"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when afc keys revoke is called for a key_id not present in config.toml, a warning is logged to stderr but exit code remains 0.
preconditions: Mock API successfully revokes the remote key
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 0; warning on stderr mentions key not found in local config
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
_, stderr = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "revoke", "cafebabe"])
assert exitCode == 0
assert stderr contains "cafebabe" OR stderr contains "not found"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that afc keys default exits with non-zero status and no config change when the specified workspace_slug has no matching [keys.*] section.
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; error message on stderr; config.toml api_key is unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
originalAPIKey = loadConfig(homeDir).APIKey
_, stderr = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "default", "nonexistent"])
assert exitCode != 0
assert stderr contains "nonexistent"
assert loadConfig(homeDir).APIKey == originalAPIKey

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that afc keys default with no argument prints usage help and exits with non-zero status.
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; usage/help message printed to stderr
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
_, stderr = runCLIWithOutput(["keys", "default"])
assert exitCode != 0
assert stderr contains "Usage" OR stderr contains "usage" OR stderr contains "workspace-slug"

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: first-time user runs afc login then executes an authenticated command without any flags, relying entirely on the auto-created config file.
smoke_tests.trigger: User runs `afc login --hub-url https://hub.example.com` from a clean environment (no existing config), then runs `afc workspace list` with no flags
real_components: OAuth login flow and callback parser
mockable: afc server OAuth endpoint (POST /api/v1/auth/callback) — returns fake user + api_key
workspace list API endpoint — returns fake workspace list

keys create exits with code 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: user revokes the default key, verifying config.toml is cleaned up and a warning is issued.

api_key is set to empty string in config.toml

smoke_tests.description: Backward compatibility smoke test: user passes --hub-url and --api-key flags and the command executes successfully without reading the config file for those values.

smoke_tests.description: Malformed config file smoke test: verifies that any afc command immediately exits with a descriptive parse error when config.toml contains invalid TOML.
smoke_tests.trigger: User runs any afc command (e.g. `afc workspace list`) when $HOME/.af/config.toml contains invalid TOML syntax
mockable: No network calls needed (command should fail before reaching API layer)
expected_effects: CLI exits with non-zero status code
Error message is printed to stderr
Error message contains the path to $HOME/.af/config.toml
Error message describes the TOML parse failure
No API calls are made
